seoTitle: How to Add a Web Widget in Zendesk to Your Site
metaDescription: Learn how to add a Zendesk Web Widget to your website, style the launcher, connect messaging channels, and embed the install script correctly.
intro: Without a chat widget, visitors have to leave your site just to ask a question. This guide shows how to configure and deploy the Zendesk Web Widget, from styling its appearance to generating and installing the JavaScript snippet on your site or Help Center.

steps.heading: Open the messaging channel settings
steps.body: In Admin Center, go to Channels and select the Messaging options panel to access your widget configuration.

steps.heading: Configure the widget wizard
steps.body: Click into your default widget configuration to set core brand links and basic setup details.

steps.heading: Style the widget
steps.body: Move to the Style tab to adjust accent colors, title text, and your logo so the widget matches your brand.

steps.heading: Connect messaging channels
steps.body: Wire up any additional channels, such as WhatsApp, Facebook, or Instagram, if you want a unified inbox behind the widget.

steps.heading: Copy the installation script
steps.body: Go to the Installation tab and copy the unique JavaScript embed snippet generated for your account.

steps.heading: Paste the code on your site
steps.body: Add the snippet before the closing body tag on your website, or enable auto-embed if you want the widget to appear on your Help Center automatically.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to add the widget?
faq.answer: No, pasting a single script snippet before the closing body tag is enough for most website builders and CMS platforms.

faq.question: Can I customize the widget's colors and logo?
faq.answer: Yes, the Style tab lets you set accent colors, title text, and upload a custom logo.

faq.question: Will the widget automatically appear on my Help Center?
faq.answer: Yes, if you enable the Zendesk Guide auto-embed option instead of manually adding the script.

faq.question: Can the widget connect to social messaging apps?
faq.answer: Yes, the messaging setup supports linking channels like WhatsApp, Facebook, and Instagram alongside the web widget.
